page: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, supported by 8.0 GB of system memory. It includes 118 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mix of capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the average range at 2268.08 and single-threaded gzip compression showing strong performance at 30.79 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are generally weak. The stress-ng multi-core CPU score is 2300.17, and large-block memory write bandwidth is poor at 11.86 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores also fall into the bottom 25% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ds_v7 offers a balanced resource density for entry-level workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and local storage, avoiding the CPU credit limitations of burstable tiers. However, the low multi-core scaling and constrained memory write bandwidth present clear tradeoffs for memory-intensive or parallel processing tasks. This server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, single-threaded file compression, and small-scale database operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, configured with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance includes 118 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded gzip compression, though multi-core execution and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. With dedicated CPU allocation under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it avoids the performance fluctuations of burstable instances. This configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for entry-level workloads that require dedicated compute resources and local disk space. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and small-scale database applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor for dedicated, entry-level workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU virtual machine with local storage, offering balanced resources and strong single-threaded compression for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Includes 118 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage
Delivers strong single-threaded gzip compression performance
Features dedicated CPU allocation via Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight web serving and small-scale database tasks
